title: Lactylation stabilizes PD-L1 to promote tumor immune evasion and cell growth
task: Elucidate lactate-mediated PD-L1 stabilization and immune evasion mechanism
key_results: Lactate lactylates PD-L1 at K280 via AARS1, stabilizing it by blocking HUWE1-mediated degradation; lactylation correlates with advanced NSCLC and poor survival
comments: Novel metabolic regulation of immune checkpoint protein; therapeutic targeting of lactylation could enhance immunotherapy efficacy.

model_io.model: tumor cell and T cell co-culture
mouse tumor model with sodium lactate

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactate upregulates PD-L1 expression via lysine lactylation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; K280 lactylation stabilizes PD-L1 by inhibiting HUWE1 binding and ubiquitination

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactylation promotes immune evasion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; lactylated PD-L1 impairs CD8+ T cell-mediated cytotoxicity and accelerates tumor growth

title: TSP-1 interaction with RANK and OPG: implications for bone remodeling and osteolytic bone metastasis
task: Characterize TSP-1 C-terminal fragment in regulating osteoclast differentiation and bone metastasis
key_results: E123CaG fragment inhibits RANKL-induced osteoclastogenesis by blocking RANK signaling and potentiating OPG; in vivo expression reduces osteolytic lesions and extends survival
comments: Dual mechanism of action (RANK inhibition + OPG stabilization) is elegant; physiological cleavage by HTRA1 suggests endogenous feedback control.

hypotheses.hypothesis: TSP-1 C-terminal fragment inhibits RANKL-induced osteoclastogenesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; E123CaG binds RANK and blocks downstream MAPK and NFATc1 signaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: E123CaG protects bone in osteolytic metastasis by dual RANK/OPG modulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; in vivo expression reduces osteolytic lesions and prolongs survival

title: An epithelial morphogenetic program for maximal urine concentration
task: Elucidate epithelial morphogenetic mechanisms enabling maximal urine concentration in ascending thin limb
key_results: Claudin-10b drives apical-junctional interdigitations that increase cell-cell contact area; claudin-10b deletion abolishes interdigitations and markedly reduces urine-concentrating ability
comments: Novel non-canonical morphogenetic role for claudin tight junction protein; elegant link between subcellular architecture and organ-level physiology.

model_io.model: ascending thin limb epithelium with single-nucleus transcriptomics and high-resolution imaging

hypotheses.hypothesis: Claudin-10b is necessary for apical-junctional interdigitation formation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; claudin-10b deletion abolishes membrane interdigitations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interdigitations are essential for maximal urine concentration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; claudin-10b knockout markedly reduces urine-concentrating ability

title: Validation of ferroptosis in zebrafish as a reliable model for phenotypic studies
task: Validate ferroptosis as a distinct cell death pathway in zebrafish and characterize its effects on vascular development
key_results: Ferroptosis successfully induced in zebrafish embryos by Erastin, FIN56, and FAC; ferroptosis significantly inhibits angiogenesis in transgenic zebrafish; differentiated from apoptosis and autophagy
comments: Zebrafish provides efficient, transparent vertebrate model for ferroptosis research with high genetic homology to humans; vascular phenotype is particularly notable.

model_io.model: zebrafish embryo treatment with ferroptosis inducers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ferroptosis can be reliably induced and measured in zebrafish embryos
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ferroptosis markers (ROS, GSH/GSSG, gene expression) detected and distinguished from apoptosis/autophagy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ferroptosis impairs angiogenesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ferroptosis significantly inhibits vascular development in transgenic zebrafish

title: SHP2 improves ovarian morphology and steroidogenic function in a rat PCOS model by modulating IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3-mediated granulosa cell pyroptosis
task: Elucidate SHP2-mediated regulation of granulosa cell pyroptosis and steroidogenic function in PCOS
key_results: SHP2 overexpression ameliorates PCOS ovarian damage (reduced cystic follicles, increased corpora lutea, normalized hormone levels); SHP2 inhibits IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 and ZEB1 pathways to suppress granulosa cell pyroptosis and apoptosis
comments: Multi-pathway targeting (IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 and ZEB1/PKP3) provides mechanistic depth; links endoplasmic reticulum stress to PCOS pathology.

model_io.model: rat PCOS model with lentiviral SHP2 overexpression
granulosa cells isolated from PCOS rats with testosterone stimulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: SHP2 overexpression ameliorates PCOS-induced ovarian pathology
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SHP2-OE reduced cystic follicles, increased corpora lutea, and normalized hormone levels in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: SHP2 suppresses granulosa cell pyroptosis through IRE1α/XBP1/NLRP3 pathway inhibition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; testosterone activated this pathway; SHP2 activity antagonized pathway activation and reduced pyroptosis markers

title: Hibernating bear serum triggers an anti-fibrotic signature in human fibroblasts, involving ECM remodeling and MAPK signaling activation
task: Identify anti-fibrotic mechanisms triggered by hibernating bear serum in human fibroblasts via ECM remodeling and MAPK signaling
key_results: Hibernating bear serum reduces fibrotic markers (COL2A1, COL11A1, COL9A1, TGFBI) and inflammatory mediators (IFI16, HMGB2), while activating MAPK cascade in human fibroblasts; active bear serum lacks this effect
comments: Untargeted proteomics reveals hibernation-specific circulating factors protective against fibrosis; potential model for tissue preservation and anti-fibrotic drug discovery.

model_io.model: human fibroblast cell culture treated with serum

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hibernating bear serum triggers an anti-fibrotic signature in human fibroblasts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; hibernating bear serum reduced fibrotic and inflammatory markers while active and control sera did not

hypotheses.hypothesis: MAPK cascade activation is associated with anti-fibrotic phenotype
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; hibernating bear serum activated MAPK pathway concurrent with reduced collagen and TGF-β-associated signaling

title: A computational rule-based model of MAPK/ERK system regulation
task: Construct rule-based computational model of MAPK/ERK pathway regulation by isoforms and 14-3-3 proteins
key_results: Model reconciles graded RAS/RAF responses with switch-like ERK output; demonstrates functional specificity of RAF isoforms through 14-3-3 interactions and selective dimer stabilization
comments: Computational approach elegantly handles combinatorial complexity; predictions about isoform-specific 14-3-3 preferences could guide experimental validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Low growth factor concentration activates RAS only in membrane subregions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; model reconciles graded upper-tier with switch-like lower-tier responses

hypotheses.hypothesis: 14-3-3 dimers confer functional differences between RAF isoforms
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 14-3-3 inhibits RAF monomers but selectively stabilizes BRAF-containing dimers

title: Targeting STAT3 pathway attenuates macrophages inflammation and cardiovascular injury in a model of Kawasaki disease
task: Investigate STAT3 pathway in macrophage-mediated inflammation and cardiovascular injury in Kawasaki disease
key_results: STAT3 phosphorylation elevated in LCWE-treated macrophages; AG490-mediated STAT3 inhibition reduces inflammatory response and cardiac injury in vivo; STAT3 blockade attenuates endothelial cell damage
comments: Clear mechanistic link between macrophage STAT3 signaling and organ injury; AG490 inhibition is pharmacologically validated target.

model_io.model: RAW264.7 macrophage and primary peritoneal macrophage cell culture
mouse coronary endothelial cell culture with macrophage-conditioned medium

hypotheses.hypothesis: STAT3 activation in macrophages drives KD-associated inflammation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; STAT3 blockade with AG490 reduces LCWE-mediated inflammatory response in vitro and cardiac injury in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: Macrophage-derived factors mediate endothelial damage through STAT3-dependent mechanisms
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; conditioned medium from STAT3-inhibited macrophages reduces endothelial cell damage

title: β-arrestin1 orchestrates endosomal signaling to regulate translational control of circadian light entrainment
task: Identify β-arrestin1-dependent endosomal signaling mechanism in circadian light entrainment
key_results: ARRB1 required for PAC1 receptor internalization and endosomal ERK1/2-RSK1-S6 cascade activation; ERK1/2-RSK1-S6 signaling enhances PER protein translation independent of transcription; endosomes identified as critical circadian signal transduction hubs
comments: Spatial specificity of endosomal signaling in circadian biology is conceptually novel; non-canonical GPCR paradigm emphasizing translational control over transcription.

model_io.model: SCN tissue and cultured neurons from ARRB1-deficient mice
behavioral circadian assay in ARRB1-deficient mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: ARRB1 is required for PAC1 internalization and endosomal signaling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ARRB1-deficient neurons show impaired PAC1 internalization and reduced endosomal ERK1/2 activation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Endosomal ERK1/2-RSK1-S6 signaling enhances PER translation for circadian entrainment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; cascade activates translation-dependent PER induction with intact transcriptional responses

title: Multi-omic identification of key transcriptional regulatory programs during endurance exercise training in rats
task: Identifying transcriptional regulatory programs during endurance exercise via multi-omic integration
key_results: Integrated analysis of chromatin accessibility, DNA methylation, mRNA, protein abundance and phosphorylation across 8 tissues (50 rats); identified tissue-specific training responses and transcription factor motifs driving coordinated epigenomic-transcriptomic changes
comments: Comprehensive multi-omics integration elegantly deconvolutes epigenomic vs. transcriptional regulation routes; sex-stratified analysis adds value for understanding sex-biased training adaptation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Endurance training induces tissue-specific transcriptional changes via distinct epigenomic and TF activity routes
hypotheses.result: Identified coordinated epigenomic-transcriptomic signatures and enriched TF motifs driving training adaptation

title: Investigating the mechanistic link between pesticide DDT and breast cancer through network toxicology, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics simulation
task: Elucidating DDT-breast cancer mechanistic links via network toxicology
key_results: Identified 12 DDT-associated breast cancer targets; core targets (AR, ESR1, ESR2, ERBB2) primarily involved in hormone and growth factor signaling
comments: Network analysis provides plausible mechanisms, but conclusions depend on database quality and require epidemiological validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DDT interacts with hormone and growth factor signaling pathways to promote breast cancer
hypotheses.result: Network analysis identified AR, ESR1, ESR2, ERBB2 as key mediators of DDT-associated breast cancer pathogenesis

title: Feature reduction using swarm optimization and random forest classifiers for early diabetes risk prediction
task: Early diabetes risk prediction with swarm-optimized feature reduction
key_results: TSO_RF (Tuna Swarm Optimization + Random Forest) achieved 100% accuracy and 98.14% mean 10-fold CV accuracy using only 14/16 features; HBA_RF reduced to 10 features with moderate performance; SHAP identified Polyuria, Polydipsia, Gender as top drivers
comments: Demonstrates effective feature reduction via metaheuristic wrapping; 100% accuracy without CV likely reflects dataset size (520) and feature redundancy, requiring external validation.

inputs: Early Stage Diabetes Risk dataset (520 individuals, 16 features)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Swarm-optimized feature selection maintains high accuracy with reduced feature burden
hypotheses.result: TSO_RF achieved 100% accuracy (no CV) and 98.14% (10-fold CV) using 87.5% of features

hypotheses.hypothesis: Polyuria, Polydipsia, and Gender are key diabetes predictors
hypotheses.result: SHAP confirmed these features have highest impact; even small changes influence predictions

title: A multi-scale adaptive filtering and AtRes_SRU–transformer synergy for breast cancer histopathology classification
task: Breast cancer histopathology classification from biomedical images
key_results: ImTranNet-TriCore achieved 95.92% accuracy, 97.83% precision (Brain MRI), 97.67% precision (Chest X-ray), outperforming CNNs, RNNs, and standalone transformers
comments: Combines learnable adaptive filtering, SRU modules, and transformers effectively, but lacks direct comparison with recent vision foundation models on same datasets.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-scale adaptive filtering plus dual-path SRU plus multi-head hybrid transformer improves classification of complex noisy biomedical data
hypotheses.result: Achieved high accuracy and precision across multiple imaging modalities with reduced false positives

title: A novel bioenergetic model outlines the metabolism of a deep-sea clam and that of its sulfur-oxidizing symbionts
task: Bioenergetic modeling of chemosymbiotic deep-sea clam and sulfur-oxidizing symbionts
key_results: Novel 'farming' DEB model reveals host maintains stable lower ingestion rather than maximal intake; most oxygen consumed by symbionts in adult clams; high host energy maintenance flux for ion homeostasis
comments: Innovative mechanistic approach to symbiosis energetics with unexpected homeostatic behavior; parameterization depends on limited published data for deep-sea organisms.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit modeling of symbiont digestion reveals novel host homeostatic strategies
hypotheses.result: Host foregoes maximal ingestion for stable lower intake; adults allocate most oxygen to symbionts

hypotheses.hypothesis: High host energy maintenance flux reflects ion homeostasis burden from symbiont metabolite release
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via model prediction of substantial proton and sulfate handling energy costs

title: Development and validation of an interpretable prediction model for the risk of unplanned reoperation in patients underwent intracranial tumor surgery
task: Predicting unplanned reoperation risk after intracranial tumor surgery using interpretable ML
key_results: Logistic regression model achieved AUC 0.836 (training) and 0.769 (internal validation); identified surgery duration, tumor location, modified Frailty Index-5, and tumor type as top predictors
comments: Well-validated single-center model with SHAP interpretability and clinical web tool; external validation on multi-center cohorts necessary.

inputs: clinical data from intracranial tumor surgery patients

hypotheses.hypothesis: Machine learning can predict unplanned reoperation risk using clinical and surgical variables
hypotheses.result: Logistic regression achieved strong AUC 0.769 on internal validation; SHAP identified 4 key drivers

title: The most important features in generalized additive models might be groups of features
task: Determining importance of feature groups in GAMs
key_results: Method enables efficient group importance analysis without retraining; identifies groups of features as primary predictors in multimodal neuroscience and health datasets
comments: Addresses a genuine gap in interpretability—joint feature effects are often overlooked—though applicability beyond GAMs remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Most significant predictors are combined effects of feature groups rather than isolated features
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in multimodal neuroscience data and social determinants of health case studies

title: Classification of fallers and non-fallers in older adults using electrical IMU signal for gait analysis and explainable deep learning
task: Fall risk classification in older adults using IMU gait signals and explainable deep learning
key_results: CNN achieved 92% fall-classification accuracy under subject-wise cross-validation; LIME analysis revealed stance phase irregularities drive 83.8% of faller classifications vs. 61.7% in non-fallers
comments: Strong deep learning performance on IMU time-series with biomechanically interpretable explanations; prospective longitudinal validation needed to confirm causality.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep learning on raw IMU stride time-series outperforms traditional approaches for fall classification
hypotheses.result: CNN achieved 92% accuracy (subject-wise CV) vs. 95% (data-level CV), confirming robust generalization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stance phase irregularities are primary drivers of fall-risk classification
hypotheses.result: LIME analysis identified 83.8% contribution in fallers vs. 61.7% in non-fallers, linked to impaired weight transfer and balance

title: Q-CaDD: accelerating in silico methodologies with quantum computation and machine learning for Epidermal growth factor receptor
task: Quantum-enhanced drug discovery for EGFR targeting using hybrid machine learning
key_results: Q-CaDD generated and screened 200,000 ligands; hybrid ensemble achieved AUC-ROC 0.83 for toxicity prediction, outperforming standalone classical models
comments: Demonstrates practical value of quantum ML in early-stage discovery despite NISQ hardware constraints; limited to NR-AR endpoint as proof-of-concept.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantum-enhanced components provide complementary predictive signals in hybrid drug discovery workflows
hypotheses.result: Hybrid ensemble achieved AUC-ROC 0.83 with competitive performance relative to classical benchmarks

title: AI-based detection of Certas Plus shunt valve settings in CT scans
task: Automated detection of CSF shunt valve settings in CT scans using deep learning
key_results: 3D U-Net successfully segmented valve components in 97.3% of test cases; correctly predicted exact or adjacent setting in 96% of cases
comments: Practical clinical tool with strong segmentation-based interpretability; limited to single valve type (Certas Plus) and single institution.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI-based segmentation of radiopaque valve components enables reliable shunt setting prediction
hypotheses.result: Model achieved 96% accuracy in predicting exact or adjacent shunt settings with interpretable segmentations

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence in intensive care units: from narrow tools to generalist systems
task: Regulatory framework for AI in intensive care units
key_results: Five-paradigm framework demonstrates regulatory complexity increases with AI functionality and scale; agentic oversight proposed for orchestrating multi-system AI
comments: Conceptual framework identifying regulatory gaps, but lacks concrete implementation details or case study validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current device-centric regulatory approaches are insufficient for generalist AI systems and large language models in ICU settings
hypotheses.result: Framework proposes agentic oversight as necessary evolution for safe orchestration of complex AI systems

title: Evolutionary digital twin framework for optimal aminoglycoside dosing in neonates with suspected sepsis
task: Optimizing aminoglycoside dosing in neonatal sepsis using digital twins
key_results: Framework calibrated on 1634 neonates; nonlinear optimal control achieved bacteriostatic exposure across all digital twins; model predictive control reduced bacterial rebound during late therapy
comments: Innovative integration of PBPK, LSTM-updated GFR, and eco-evolutionary pharmacodynamics, though clinical validation remains needed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: In silico optimization balancing efficacy and safety accounts for resistance-driven MIC changes
hypotheses.result: Nonlinear optimal control and model predictive control strategies successfully maintained therapeutic exposure

title: High-sensitivity pan-cancer AI assessment of lymph node metastasis via uncertainty quantification
task: Pan-cancer lymph node metastasis assessment with uncertainty quantification
key_results: UPATHLN achieved AUC 0.986 on internal validation; uncertainty module achieved 100% conditional sensitivity by flagging all false negatives for review; reduced negative node review burden by 73.2%
comments: Exemplary safety-critical AI design integrating foundation models with uncertainty—achieves 100% sensitivity on held-out tumor origins through explicit fail-safe mechanism.

inputs: lymph node histopathology images (26,229 from 14 primary sites)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit uncertainty quantification in AI prevents overconfident misclassification on rare tumor variants
hypotheses.result: Uncertainty module intercepted all false negatives; achieved 100% conditional sensitivity even on unseen tumor origins

title: Dynamic channel allocation for secondary users in cognitive radio network
task: Dynamic spectrum allocation for secondary users in cognitive radio networks via fuzzy logic
key_results: 27-rule Mamdani fuzzy logic model improves throughput, reduces service delay and drop rate, increases spectrum utilization vs. traditional CRN approaches; distributed SU decision-making enables adaptive responsiveness
comments: Fuzzy approach is sensible for uncertain channel conditions, but paper lacks quantitative benchmarks (e.g., % improvements) and comparison against other adaptive allocation schemes.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fuzzy-based dynamic allocation outperforms traditional fixed allocation
hypotheses.result: Improved throughput, reduced delay/drop rate, increased spectrum utilization in MATLAB simulations

title: Analytical analysis of the nonlinear fractional order Pochhammer-Chree equation with power-law nonlinearity in elastic materials
task: Solve nonlinear fractional Pochhammer-Chree equation with power-law nonlinearity
key_results: Kumar-Malik method yields bright/dark soliton and kink solutions; fractional parameter χ controls soliton shape, width, and amplitude
comments: Analytical treatment of fractional PDEs is well-established; visual validation via Mathematica plots provides limited quantitative verification of solution accuracy.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Kumar-Malik method efficiently solves fractional Pochhammer-Chree equation
hypotheses.result: Method generates accurate bright/dark soliton and kink waveforms with χ-dependent dynamics

title: Effects of AI-assisted review presentation formats on consumer decision-making efficiency from a cognitive load perspective
task: Quantify impact of AI review presentation format on cognitive load and movie purchase decisions
key_results: Bullet-point reviews reduce cognitive load by 11.8% in high-demand narrative contexts; no significant effect in low-demand contexts; cognitive load mediates format-decision link
comments: Well-motivated experimental design grounded in cognitive load theory, but effect size (11.8% reduction) is modest and requires replication; lack of behavioral outcome (purchase/watch intention) weakens practical relevance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bullet-point format reduces cognitive load more in high-demand than low-demand contexts
hypotheses.result: 11.8% reduction in high-demand; no significant difference in low-demand

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cognitive load mediates the effect of format on decision outcomes
hypotheses.result: Moderated mediation by task complexity confirmed

title: Skilled personnel risk management under war-induced forced migration: implications for labor market sustainability
task: Assess workforce risk from war-induced migration using Human Capital Risk Index and Monte Carlo forecasting
key_results: Poland and Czechia face highest labor market risks (HCRI 0.0372, 0.0419); Germany absorbs refugee workforce despite largest inflows; Monte Carlo sims show volatility in smaller economies, resilience in developed markets
comments: Methodologically sound integration of risk metrics and stochastic modeling, but HCRI validation and empirical grounding (surveys/interviews noted as missing) limit policy credibility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Smaller economies face higher labor market risk from refugee inflows
hypotheses.result: Poland, Czechia show HCRI >0.037; Germany resilient despite largest inflows

hypotheses.hypothesis: Workforce volatility decreases with economic development
hypotheses.result: Developed markets (Germany, UK) demonstrate greater absorption capacity in Monte Carlo forecasts
